title: Java Basics
description: Test your knowledge of Java fundamentals including syntax, variables, and core data types

questions.question: Which method is the entry point of a Java program?
questions.explanation: Java applications start execution from the main method.

questions.question: Which keyword is used to create a class in Java?
questions.explanation: Java uses the class keyword to define classes.

questions.question: Which of these is a valid Java variable declaration?
questions.explanation: Java declares typed variables using type, name, and optional assignment.

questions.question: Which primitive type stores whole numbers in Java?
questions.explanation: int is the standard integer primitive type in Java.

questions.question: How do you write a single-line comment in Java?
Which of these is not a Java primitive type?
questions.explanation: String is a class, not a primitive type.

questions.question: What does System.out.println() do?
options: Prints output with a new line
questions.explanation: System.out.println prints text and moves the cursor to the next line.

questions.question: Which operator is used for assignment in Java?
questions.explanation: A single equals sign is used for assignment.

questions.question: What is the size of a Java char?
questions.explanation: Java char is a 16-bit Unicode code unit.

questions.question: Which keyword is used to define a constant in Java?
questions.explanation: final is used to prevent reassignment of variables.
